Fagopyrum esculentum, or buckwheat, is a pseudo-cereal plant that belongs to the Polygonaceae family. It is a nutrient-rich plant that provides several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. The bioactive constituents of buckwheat include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tartaric acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Buckwheat is commonly prepared as a tea, flour, or extract, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
